page: Amazon Web Services x8aedz.metal-12xl is a bare-metal server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9R05 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 5.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 1536.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided via a 3800 GB NVMe SSD, and network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 38 Gbps. No graphics processing units are included in this configuration.

Benchmark evaluations place this instance in the top tier for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance, as measured by stress-ng and Geekbench. Memory subsystem performance is characterized by a read-only small-block cached bandwidth of 11,419.53 GB/sec and a large-block uncached read bandwidth of 536.36 GB/sec. Database and in-memory operations demonstrate high throughput, with Redis SET operations reaching 176.61 million operations per second and Passmark database operations scoring in the top tier. Additionally, the system achieves a static web serving throughput of 118.26 GB/sec and processes LLM inference tasks efficiently, generating 84.98 tokens per second on a medium 7B model.

From a resource density perspective, the x8aedz.metal-12xl offers high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads due to its massive 1.5 TB RAM capacity relative to its 48 physical cores. However, the lack of hardware GPU acceleration represents a clear tradeoff, limiting its utility for heavy parallel deep learning training, though it remains capable of CPU-based LLM inference. The combination of high-frequency dedicated AMD EPYC cores, substantial NVMe SSD storage, and extensive memory capacity makes this server type suitable for large-scale in-memory databases, intensive data analytics, enterprise database management systems, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services x8aedz.metal-12xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by an AMD EPYC 9R05 x86_64 processor with 48 dedicated physical cores running at 5.0 GHz. It features 1536.0 GB of RAM, providing a high density of 32.0 GB per core, alongside a 3800 GB local NVMe SSD and a 38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Lacking GPU hardware, the instance relies on its high-frequency CPU and memory architecture, achieving top-tier performance in multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations. Benchmark results show 176.61 million Redis SET operations per second and 118.26 GB/sec static web serving throughput. This configuration offers high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive applications without requiring GPU acceleration. It is designed for large-scale in-memory databases, heavy data processing, enterprise database management systems, and high-throughput web serving workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x8aedz.metal-12xl is a bare-metal server with 48 AMD EPYC cores, 1536 GB RAM, and a 3800 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x8aedz.metal-12xl is a bare-metal server featuring 48 AMD EPYC cores, 1.5 TB RAM, and a 3800 GB NVMe SSD for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency bare-metal platform combining massive memory capacity with local NVMe storage for demanding database and in-memory workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R05 physical cores running at 5.0 GHz
1536.0 GB of system memory providing 32.0 GB per core
3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier memory bandwidth and database processing performance
Suited for in-memory databases, large-scale data analytics, and web serving
